# portcourt — demo transcript

Every block below is the **real, captured output** of running portcourt against the fixtures in [`examples/`](.). It is regenerated by running the tool and is staleness-gated, so it cannot lie about what portcourt prints.

### `$ portcourt report examples/honest.toml`

```text
module            parity  ported missing doc-only disabled
------------------------------------------------------------
alpha.c           100.0%       2       0        1        0
beta.c             33.3%       1       1        1        0
------------------------------------------------------------
TOTAL              50.0%       3       1        2        0
```
_exit code: 0_

### `$ portcourt next beta.c examples/honest.toml`

```text
beta.c — 1 missing, 1 doc-only
  missing : b_write
  doc-only: b_flush (reword the comment that names these)
```
_exit code: 0_

### `$ portcourt explain b_write examples/honest.toml`

```text
portcourt explain `b_write`
  file:   beta.c
  status: MISSING (no Rust fn)
  courts: COURT.BETA.OPEN
```
_exit code: 0_

### `$ portcourt check examples/honest.toml`

```text
portcourt check — closure math over 2 declared module(s)

  [ok  ] alpha.c        100.0%  ported   2  missing   0  doc-only  1  (1 required court(s))
  [ok  ] beta.c          33.3%  ported   1  missing   1  doc-only  1  (0 required court(s))

PORTCOURT: PASS — 2 module claim(s) all backed by evidence.
```
_exit code: 0_

### `$ portcourt check examples/overclaim.toml`

```text
portcourt check — closure math over 2 declared module(s)

  [FAIL] alpha.c        100.0%  ported   2  missing   0  doc-only  1  (1 required court(s))
         - requires court `COURT.BETA.OPEN` which is not sealed
  [FAIL] beta.c          33.3%  ported   1  missing   1  doc-only  1  (0 required court(s))
         - claims `complete` but has 1 missing + 1 doc-only on compiled functions (over-claim)

PORTCOURT: FAIL — 2 over-claim(s):
  ✗ alpha.c: requires court `COURT.BETA.OPEN` which is not sealed
  ✗ beta.c: claims `complete` but has 1 missing + 1 doc-only on compiled functions (over-claim)
```
_exit code: 1_
